data.title: CDT Equity Increases Bitcoin Holdings with Recent Purchase
data.description: According to BlockBeats, CDT Equity, a publicly traded company on the U.S. stock market, has announced an increase in its Bitcoin holdings. The company acquired approximately 9.25 Bitcoins at an average purchase price of $108,301.75 each. This acquisition brings CDT Equity's total Bitcoin holdings to approximately 17.9 Bitcoins.

data.title: A whale withdrew 20.8 million USDT from Aster and transferred it to Hyperliquid, and then purchased 143.98 BTC.
data.description: According to MLM monitoring, the whale who sold 5.07 million HYPE tokens three weeks ago and transferred part of them to Aster withdrew 2,080 USDT from Aster and transferred it to Hyperliquid in the past few hours, and then purchased 143.98 BTC (worth $16.7 million).

data.title: The value of Gate Alpha's 74th ZBT airdrop has reached $260
data.description: According to official news, Gate Alpha will launch its 74th ZBT airdrop at 9:00 PM (UTC+8) tonight, with the highest airdrop value reaching $260. ZBT's price has reportedly risen to $1 since its launch. Gate Alpha currently supports public chains including SOL, ETH, BNB Chain, Base, SUI, ARB, World Chain, AVAX, POLY, LINEA, ZK, and OP.

data.title: OpenSea is transforming into a crypto trading aggregator, with crypto trading volume reaching $1.6 billion so far in October.
data.description: OpenSea is undergoing a difficult transformation, expanding from an NFT business to a one-stop trading platform for all cryptocurrencies on 22 different blockchains. Data shows that in the first two weeks of October 2025, OpenSea facilitated $1.6 billion worth of cryptocurrency transactions and $230 million worth of NFT transactions, far higher than the total trading volume of $142 million in May. This growth will make October 2025 the largest month in terms of trading volume in more than three years. Under OpenSea's new plan, it integrates buy and sell orders from decentralized cryptocurrency exchanges such as Uniswap and Meteora. In terms of fees, OpenSea charges a fee of approximately 0.9% for each transaction, generating $16 million in revenue in the past two weeks. (Forbes)

data.title: Tether officially opens source wallet development kit WDK
data.description: According to official news, Tether announced the official open source wallet development kit WDK, a new development toolkit designed to enable humans, machines and artificial intelligence agents to build, deploy and use secure, multi-chain, self-hosted wallets that can be integrated into any device, from embedded systems to mobile, desktop or server operating systems.

data.title: FIFA World Cup NFT platform faces criminal complaint from Swiss regulator
data.description: Swiss regulator GESPA has filed a criminal complaint with prosecutors, alleging that the FIFA World Cup NFT platform violated national gambling laws. The investigation revealed that the FIFA Collect platform provided an unlicensed gambling service in Switzerland, where participants paid money and could win prizes through a random draw, constituting an illegal operation. FIFA did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the incident. (The Block)

data.title: Binance Wallet ZBT Booster Season 1 Rewards Now Available
data.description: Binance Wallet announced on the X platform that the first season of the ZEROBASE (ZBT) Booster event is now open for redemption, with a total of 20 million ZBT rewards.

data.title: USDD Smart Allocator’s cumulative revenue has exceeded US$5 million
data.description: The decentralized stablecoin USDD has officially announced that its Smart Allocator mechanism has generated over $5 million in real returns. Smart Allocator is a yield-generating strategy for USDD that intelligently allocates protocol reserve assets to DeFi platforms like Aave and JustLend, enabling sustainable yield generation. Currently, USDD has a TVL exceeding $550 million and has been deployed on major public chains such as TRON and Ethereum.

data.title: White House economic adviser Hassett: The expected three rate cuts are just the beginning
data.description: White House economic adviser Hassett said the Fed's three interest rate cuts are only the beginning, and that banks currently have ample reserves, so there's no reason they can't continue to maintain a 4% growth rate. (Jinshi)

data.title: The total long position of Andrew Kang's associated address has increased to $84.21 million, with a current floating profit of $720,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa), an address possibly associated with Mechanism Capital co-founder Andrew Kang has increased its total ETH position to $84.21 million after shifting from short positions to long ETH, with a current floating profit of $720,000. Over the past few hours, Kang has increased his ETH long position by 25x to $28.88 million, increased his ENA long position by 10x to $6.11 million, and opened new long positions in SOL, BTC, and HYPE, bringing his total position to $49.25 million.

data.title: A whale trader sold 7,818 ETH at $3,714, resulting in a $3.47 million loss.
data.description: According to monitoring by on-chain analyst Ember, a whale who bought high and sold low sold 7,818 ETH at $3,714, resulting in a loss of $3.47 million. In half a month, this whale successfully turned 38.017 million DAI into $29.038 million through two waves of buying high and selling low, resulting in a loss of $8.979 million.

data.title: Orochi Network Completes $8 Million in New Funding, with Ethereum Foundation and Others Joining
data.description: Orochi Network, a decentralized data infrastructure project, announced the completion of a new round of financing of US$8 million, with participation from institutions such as the Ethereum Foundation. As of now, its total financing has reached US$20 million. The new funds will be used to build verifiable data infrastructure for areas such as RWA and stablecoins.

data.title: Cryptoquant: Short-term fluctuations do not necessarily mean there are structural problems in the market; long-term investors will enhance market resilience
data.description: Analysts at Cryptoquant say the market is facing shocks similar to those of 2021, but with a different structure. In previous cycles, panic phases saw increased reserves on exchanges, and the influx of liquidity on exchanges amplified selling pressure. Bitcoin balances on exchanges are now at a decade-low, indicating limited supply available for sale and a tighter market structure. With fewer Bitcoins held on exchanges, a sustained downward trend is unlikely. The behavior of long-term holders is also different. In 2020 and 2021, the long-term holder SOPR (LTH-SOPR) remained well below 1 for several months, indicating investors feared losses from the exits. However, during this decline, the ratio remained near neutral, indicating cautious profit-taking rather than fear-driven selling. Long-term investors continued to hold on during the volatility, strengthening market resilience. Looking back at past shocks, the March 2020 crash eliminated leverage, leading to significant accumulation by large investors and resulting in a V-shaped rebound. In May 2021, Bitcoin plummeted 30% due to the impact of Tesla and regulatory pressure. Large investors sold approximately 50,000 Bitcoins, subsequently repurchasing 34,000 at the bottom. In August 2023, a US debt downgrade triggered a 15% correction, briefly dipping the SOPR before quickly rebounding. Each of these events cleared out excessive leverage and ushered in a new round of accumulation. This downturn has made the market appear more mature. With dwindling reserves on trading platforms and a stable long-term holder base, temporary fluctuations do not equate to structural weaknesses, and Bitcoin is laying the foundation for its next upcycle.

data.title: Over the past hour, the entire network has experienced a liquidation of over $54 million, mainly due to short positions.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network had a liquidation of US$54.34141 million, of which short positions had a liquidation of US$50.2163 million and long positions had a liquidation of US$4.1278 million.

data.description:
Jamie Dimon warned about “cockroaches” in the US economy after JPMorgan took a $170 million hit from Tricolor’s collapse.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jamie-dimon-cockroaches-in-the-us-economy/

data.title: Planet Evening News | October 17
Japan’s big banks join forces on stablecoins to speed corporate payments: Nikkei
data.description:
Japan’s MUFG, SMBC and Mizuho plan to jointly issue yen and dollar-pegged stablecoins in a push to standardize corporate onchain payments.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/375083/japans-big-banks-join-forces-on-stablecoins-to-speed-corporate-payments-nikkei?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Breaking: Binance Among Crypto Exchanges Facing AML Probe in France
data.description:
Key Insights: Binance and other crypto exchanges are facing a broader anti-money laundering (AML) and compliance framework, and risk control checks as part of broader oversight across exchanges in France, according to sources.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/10/17/breaking-binance-among-crypto-exchanges-facing-aml-probe-in-france/

data.title: OKX to Launch ZBT (ZEROBASE) Pre-Market Trading
data.description: According to official news, OKX will launch pre-market trading of ZBT (ZEROBASE) at 7:30 PM (UTC+8) on October 17, 2025. ZEROBASE is a decentralized cryptographic infrastructure network that enables verifiable off-chain computations through zero-knowledge proofs and a trusted execution environment.
